name: Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the sliced dried figs, chopped dates, prunes, crushed pineapple, dark raisins, chopped apple, and chopped walnuts.
2. Add 1/2 cup orange juice to the mixture.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup whole wheat flour, 1/2 cup toasted wheat germ, 1 tablespoon baking powder, 1 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon, and 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the fruit mixture, stirring until well combined.
5. Stir in 2 eggs.
6. Pour the batter into a lightly oiled 10-inch tube pan.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 40 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Turn off the heat and let cool in the pan for 15 minutes before removing it.
1/15: This recipe yields approximately one fruitcake (193 calories), one bread, one and a half fruits, and one fat exchange with 37 grams of carbohydrates, 5 grams of protein, 4 grams of fat, 117 milligrams of sodium, and 349 milligrams of potassium. Cholesterol content is 37 mg.
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986 (Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93)
